clean_story: Stronger from stress.  Chaos as fuel. "Antifragile" isn’t just tough—it’s the opposite of fragile. Fragile things break under pressure. Robust things resist it. Antifragile things need it to grow. Think of muscles. Lift weights, they tear—then rebuild thicker. No stress, they shrink. Or forests. Small fires clear deadwood, letting sunlight nourish new shoots. Prevent all fires, and one day a wildfire consumes everything. Systems hide this trait. Your immune system strengthens after fighting a virus. Startups pivot from failures into breakthroughs. Even myths know it:Hydra grows two heads for each one cut off. The trick?Volatility isn’t noise—it’s data. Bones adapt to impact. Cities decentralize after disasters. Antifragile entities don’t just survive shocks—they steal energy from them. Three related subjects are adaptive resilience, stochastic benefits, and hormesis effects.
